Oh my God, I can’t put into words how deeply this documentary has moved me. Ocean Ramsey is a true warrior for animal welfare, and I felt so connected to her fight.

Today, July 5th, from Pamplona (Spain), I’m suffering in anticipation of the San Fermín festival, which I know will bring, in two days, unbearable blood and suffering from the animals who will die at the hands of people in an arena, and from people like me who can’t bear the pain of knowing that this archaic tradition still exists.
I feel trapped in the Middle Ages, powerless, not knowing how to cope with it every year.

This documentary made me cry, but above all, it warmed my heart and gave me hope.

PLEASE, PEOPLE LIKE YOU ❤ To you, and everyone reading this: let’s unite against injustices like this and make them disappear.

⚠️ To everyone reading my message who wants to help a whole country: please send a message to the City Council of Pamplona, demanding the abolition of bullfighting, once and for all. There’s more pressure every year, we definitely can make this happen.

Like the vast majority of people in Spain, I've been suffering through this so-called "spectacle", since I was a little girl. They show it on national TV, people pay to watch animals die, with celebration music around them. Now I’m 26, and it hasn’t gotten easier, only heavier to keep enduring.

Thank you, truly. Sending a huge hug to all of you, because I know the world is full of amazing and loving people, we just need to raise our voices and be heard 🫂❤️‍🩹.

I had the pleasure of diving with Ocean in the Maldives on a tiger shark interaction and freediving training course several years ago. As a professional Divemaster and marine conservationist, I went in very skeptical of her approach and "doing it for the gram" but left entirely convinced this woman is a true activist through and through. What she does is controvertial, and sharks should never be portrayed as safe innocent animals (and she always makes this clear in her videos), but ultimately her advocacy has changed hearts, mind, and laws (in Hawaii). If this approach can make a difference to the lives of 100M+ sharks killed per year then I am all for it. Go Ocean!

What is this movie really about? It strikes me as soft porn, the narrator’s agenda being to use the sharks as click bait for promoting her Barbie-doll allure. 

Years ago, thanks to Warner Herzog, we witnessed the nearly inevitable consequence of self-promotion via proximity to dangerous animals the narrator wrongly senses they have bonded with. That narrator was Timothy Treadwell. The film was “Grizzly Man,” and in it the famed director explores the narrator’s true subject matter, himself. 

I do not think Herzog would be interested in devoting his exceptional story-telling skills to documenting the Shark Bimbo. There’s no there, there.
